complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Project Setup and Structure task into 3 subtasks: 1) Gradle configuration and dependency setup, 2) Directory structure creation and initial file setup, and 3) Documentation and verification. For each subtask, include specific steps, acceptance criteria, and estimated time.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-low complexity as it involves standard project setup procedures with clear requirements. The task is well-defined with specific steps but requires careful attention to configuration details to ensure proper JavaFX integration.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Create Base Model Classes task into 4 subtasks: 1) Implement abstract Account class with core functionality, 2) Create concrete account type implementations (SavingsAccount, CreditAccount), 3) Implement User class and authentication model, and 4) Develop exception handling for account operations. For each subtask, include specific implementation details and unit test requirements.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity as it requires designing a proper inheritance hierarchy and implementing business logic for different account types. The task involves core domain modeling that will impact many other components.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the In-Memory Data Storage task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design and implement the DataStore singleton class with collection management, 2) Create the Transaction class with all required properties and methods, 3) Implement data access methods with proper validation and exception handling, and 4) Develop search and filtering functionality for accounts and transactions. For each subtask, include specific implementation details and test cases.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it requires implementing a data management system with proper validation, search capabilities, and exception handling. The singleton pattern adds complexity, and this component will be critical for application functionality.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Create Basic UI Components task into 5 subtasks: 1) Design and implement main application window and navigation framework, 2) Create account listing and details views, 3) Develop transaction form and history views, 4) Implement CSS styling and responsive design, and 5) Set up resource loading and view navigation system. For each subtask, include specific UI requirements, layout details, and testing approach.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to design and implement multiple UI components with proper navigation, styling, and responsiveness. JavaFX FXML integration adds complexity, and UI work typically requires significant iteration and refinement.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Account Management Controllers task into 4 subtasks: 1) Implement AccountController with core account management methods, 2) Create form validation system for account operations, 3) Develop UI-model data binding and formatting utilities, and 4) Implement error handling and user feedback mechanisms. For each subtask, include specific controller methods, validation rules, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it connects UI components to the model layer and requires implementing validation logic, error handling, and proper MVC separation. This controller will handle critical business operations and needs robust implementation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Transaction Processing task into 5 subtasks: 1) Implement TransactionController with deposit and withdrawal methods, 2) Create transaction validation system with business rules, 3) Develop transaction history display with filtering and sorting, 4) Implement receipt generation functionality, and 5) Create error handling for transaction edge cases. For each subtask, include specific implementation details, validation rules, and test scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement critical financial transaction logic with proper validation, error handling, and edge case management. Transaction processing is a core banking function requiring high reliability and thorough testing.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the SQLite Database Integration task into 5 subtasks: 1) Configure SQLite dependency and create database schema design, 2) Implement DatabaseManager with connection pooling, 3) Create SQL scripts for database initialization, 4) Develop database utility methods for common operations, and 5) Implement comprehensive error handling for database operations. For each subtask, include specific implementation details, SQL scripts, and testing approach.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity as it involves setting up a persistent storage system with proper connection management, schema design, and error handling. Database integration is a critical infrastructure component requiring careful implementation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the JPA Entities task into 4 subtasks: 1) Configure JPA/Hibernate dependencies and persistence.xml, 2) Implement base entity classes with JPA annotations, 3) Create entity inheritance hierarchy for account types, and 4) Implement entity relationships and constraints. For each subtask, include specific entity designs, annotation details, and testing requirements.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity due to the need to properly implement JPA entity mapping with inheritance, relationships, and constraints. Entity design decisions will significantly impact database structure and application performance.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Repository Layer task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design repository interfaces for all entity types, 2) Implement JPA-based repository implementations with CRUD operations, 3) Create custom query methods for specific business requirements, and 4) Implement transaction management and error handling. For each subtask, include specific repository methods, query implementations, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it requires implementing data access patterns with proper transaction management, error handling, and custom queries. The repository layer is critical for data integrity and performance.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Service Layer task into 5 subtasks: 1) Design service interfaces for all business domains, 2) Implement account management services with business logic, 3) Create transaction processing services with validation rules, 4) Develop user management services with security features, and 5) Implement cross-cutting concerns like logging and exception handling. For each subtask, include specific service methods, business rules, and testing requirements.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity as it encapsulates core business logic, validation rules, and coordinates between controllers and repositories. The service layer implements critical business rules and requires careful design and thorough testing.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Data Migration Tool task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design migration workflow and data mapping strategy, 2) Implement data extraction from in-memory storage, 3) Create database persistence with validation and error handling, and 4) Develop progress tracking and reporting functionality. For each subtask, include specific implementation details, validation rules, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to reliably transfer data between storage systems with proper validation, error handling, and rollback capabilities. Data migration requires careful handling to prevent data loss or corruption.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the User Authentication System task into 5 subtasks: 1) Implement secure password hashing and verification, 2) Create login controller and UI components, 3) Develop authentication service with session creation, 4) Implement security features like login attempt limiting, and 5) Create remember-me functionality and session persistence. For each subtask, include specific security requirements, implementation details, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to security implications and the need for robust implementation of authentication mechanisms, password hashing, and session management. Security features require careful implementation and thorough testing.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the User Management task into 4 subtasks: 1) Create user administration UI and controller, 2) Implement CRUD operations for user accounts, 3) Develop role-based access control system, and 4) Create user profile management functionality. For each subtask, include specific implementation details, security requirements, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity as it involves implementing user administration features with proper security controls, role management, and validation. User management requires careful handling of permissions and security concerns.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Session Management task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design session data structure and storage mechanism, 2) Implement session creation, validation, and timeout functionality, 3) Create session renewal and activity tracking, and 4) Develop secure logout and session termination. For each subtask, include specific security requirements, implementation details, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to security implications and the need to properly manage user sessions with timeout handling, secure storage, and proper cleanup. Session management is critical for application security.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Interest Calculation for Savings Accounts
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Interest Calculation task into 4 subtasks: 1) Enhance savings account model with interest properties, 2) Implement daily interest accrual algorithm, 3) Create monthly interest posting and transaction recording, and 4) Develop interest rate management and calculation history. For each subtask, include specific calculation formulas, business rules, and testing scenarios with various interest rates and balances.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need for precise financial calculations, scheduling, and transaction recording. Interest calculation requires careful implementation to ensure accuracy and proper financial handling.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Recurring Transactions task into 5 subtasks: 1) Design recurring transaction model and scheduling system, 2) Implement transaction frequency and execution rules, 3) Create transaction execution engine with proper validation, 4) Develop modification and cancellation functionality, and 5) Implement notification system for upcoming transactions. For each subtask, include specific scheduling algorithms, validation rules, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement a reliable scheduling system with proper execution, validation, and modification capabilities. Recurring transactions require careful handling of timing and execution states.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the PDF Statement Generation task into 4 subtasks: 1) Integrate PDF generation library and create document templates, 2) Implement statement data collection and formatting, 3) Create statement generation scheduling and storage, and 4) Develop statement delivery options (download, email). For each subtask, include specific implementation details, document design requirements, and testing approach.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to generate properly formatted financial documents with accurate data representation. PDF generation requires careful handling of layout, data formatting, and document structure.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Dynamic Credit Limits task into 5 subtasks: 1) Enhance credit account model with history tracking, 2) Implement credit score calculation algorithm, 3) Create automatic limit adjustment rules, 4) Develop approval workflow for limit changes, and 5) Implement notification and reporting system. For each subtask, include specific algorithms, business rules, and testing scenarios with various credit histories.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement sophisticated credit scoring algorithms, approval workflows, and automatic adjustments. Credit limit management requires careful implementation of financial rules and risk assessment.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Additional Account Types task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design and implement checking account model with specific features, 2) Create investment account model with unique properties, 3) Update service and repository layers to support new account types, and 4) Implement UI components for new account management. For each subtask, include specific account features, business rules, and testing requirements.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it requires extending the existing account hierarchy with new types having unique business rules and properties. This task impacts multiple layers of the application and requires careful integration.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Account Transfers task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design transfer model and transaction logic, 2) Implement transfer validation and fee calculation, 3) Create transfer limits and history tracking, and 4) Develop transfer UI components and scheduled transfers. For each subtask, include specific validation rules, fee structures, and testing scenarios with various account types.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement reliable transfer mechanisms between accounts with proper validation, fee calculation, and transaction recording. Transfers require careful handling of financial transactions across accounts.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Transaction Categorization task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design category model and relationship to transactions, 2) Implement category management service and UI, 3) Create automatic categorization rules and algorithms, and 4) Develop category-based reporting and budgeting features. For each subtask, include specific categorization rules, UI requirements, and testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement both manual and automatic categorization systems with reporting capabilities. Categorization requires sophisticated pattern matching and rule processing.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Advanced Transaction Search task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design search criteria model and query builder, 2) Implement search execution with pagination and sorting, 3) Create search history and result management, and 4) Develop advanced search UI with export capabilities. For each subtask, include specific search parameters, query optimization techniques, and testing scenarios with large datasets.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement a flexible search system with advanced filtering, pagination, and sorting capabilities. Search functionality requires careful query optimization and result handling.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Dashboard View task into 4 subtasks: 1) Design dashboard layout and widget framework, 2) Implement account summary and transaction list components, 3) Create alert notification system and quick actions, and 4) Develop dashboard customization and refresh functionality. For each subtask, include specific UI requirements, data aggregation methods, and testing approach.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it requires implementing a comprehensive dashboard with multiple data visualization components, alerts, and customization options. Dashboard implementation requires careful UI design and data aggregation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Charts and Graphs task into 4 subtasks: 1) Integrate chart libraries and create data preparation service, 2) Implement balance and transaction history visualizations, 3) Create category and comparison chart components, and 4) Develop chart customization and export functionality. For each subtask, include specific chart types, data aggregation methods, and testing scenarios with various data patterns.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to the need to implement sophisticated data visualization components with proper data aggregation, formatting, and interactivity. Chart implementation requires careful handling of data transformation and visual presentation.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Dark Mode and Accessibility Features
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Dark Mode and Accessibility Features task into 4 subtasks: 1) Implement theme switching system and preference storage, 2) Create light and dark CSS themes with consistent styling, 3) Develop accessibility features like keyboard navigation and screen reader support, and 4) Implement font size adjustment and color blindness accommodations. For each subtask, include specific UI requirements, accessibility standards, and testing approach with various user needs.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity as it requires implementing comprehensive theming and accessibility features across the entire application. Accessibility implementation requires careful attention to standards and diverse user needs.
